facts.text: Opa‑locka was founded in 1926 by aviation pioneer Glenn Curtiss, who imagined a city inspired by Arabian Nights tales.
The city boasts one of the largest collections of Moorish‑Revival architecture in the Western Hemisphere, with streets named after Sinbad, Ali Baba, and Sultan.
Despite the 1926 hurricane that halted the Florida land boom, Opa‑locka continued building; by year‑end 93 structures were complete or under construction.
Twenty buildings in Opa‑locka are listed on the National Register of Historic Places, all part of the city’s Moorish‑Thematic Resource Area.
The 1926 opening of Opa‑locka’s train station, dubbed the “grand Vizier of the Sheikdom,” launched the famed Orange Blossom Special passenger train.
HistoryMiami’s Special Collections house a near‑complete archive of Opa‑locka’s architectural plans, sketches, and photographs from the city’s founding era.
